    <h1 {...{
      "id": "brand-concierge-implementation-guide-android"
    }}>{`Brand Concierge Implementation Guide (Android)`}</h1>
    <p>{`The Brand Concierge extension embeds a conversational chat UI into your host app. It uses AEP SDK shared state from Mobile Core, Edge, and Edge Identity to configure and run a session.`}</p>
    <p>{`The Brand Concierge UI has two integration approaches:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Managed Integration`}</strong>{`: Provides a drop-in entry point to the chat interface and lets the Brand Concierge extension automatically manage it.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Custom Integration`}</strong>{`: Embeds and manages the chat UI directly into your app's view hierarchy for dedicated chat screens or custom layouts.`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p>{`Both approaches are available for Compose and XML/Views-based apps.`}</p>
    <hr></hr>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "prerequisites"
    }}>{`Prerequisites`}</h2>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "required-sdk-modules"
    }}>{`Required SDK modules`}</h3>
    <p>{`Your app needs the following Experience Platform SDKs to be available and registered:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><a parentName="li" {...{
          "href": "https://github.com/adobe/aepsdk-core-android"
        }}>{`Mobile Core`}</a></li>
      <li parentName="ul"><a parentName="li" {...{
          "href": "https://github.com/adobe/aepsdk-edgeidentity-android"
        }}>{`Edge Identity`}</a></li>
      <li parentName="ul"><a parentName="li" {...{
          "href": "https://github.com/adobe/aepsdk-concierge-android"
        }}>{`Brand Concierge`}</a></li>
    </ul>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "android-version"
    }}>{`Android version`}</h3>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Minimum Android API level 24 (Android 7.0) or higher`}</li>
    </ul>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "permissions-for-speech-to-text-optional"
    }}>{`Permissions for speech to text (optional)`}</h3>
    <p>{`Speech to text uses Android Speech Recognition APIs and microphone APIs for voice input functionality. Add this permission to your app `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`AndroidManifest.xml`}</inlineCode>{`:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-xml"
      }}>{`<uses-permission android:name="android.permission.RECORD_AUDIO" />
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`The SDK handles permission requests internally when users interact with the microphone button.`}</p>
    <hr></hr>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "installation"
    }}>{`Installation`}</h2>
    <p>{`Add the dependencies to your app module's `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`build.gradle.kts`}</inlineCode>{`:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-kotlin"
      }}>{`dependencies {
    implementation("com.adobe.marketing.mobile:concierge:3.+")
    implementation("com.adobe.marketing.mobile:core:3.5.0")
    implementation("com.adobe.marketing.mobile:edgeidentity:3.0.0")
}
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`Then sync your project with the Gradle files.`}</p>
    <hr></hr>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "configuration"
    }}>{`Configuration`}</h2>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "step-1-register-the-brand-concierge-extension"
    }}>{`Step 1: Register the Brand Concierge extension`}</h3>
    <p>{`Import and register the extensions in your `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`Application`}</inlineCode>{` class `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`onCreate()`}</inlineCode>{`:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-kotlin"
      }}>{`import com.adobe.marketing.mobile.MobileCore
import com.adobe.marketing.mobile.Concierge
import com.adobe.marketing.mobile.edge.identity.Identity as EdgeIdentity
import android.app.Application

class MainApp : Application() {
    override fun onCreate() {
        super.onCreate()
        MobileCore.setApplication(this)
        MobileCore.initialize(this, "YOUR_APP_ID")
    }
}
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`Replace `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`YOUR_APP_ID`}</inlineCode>{` with your mobile property App ID from Adobe Data Collection. For full setup instructions see the `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "/aep-mobile-sdkdocs/src/pages/home/getting-started/"
      }}>{`Adobe Experience Platform Mobile SDK getting started guide`}</a>{`.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "step-2-validate-the-brand-concierge-configuration-keys-exist"
    }}>{`Step 2: Validate the Brand Concierge configuration keys exist`}</h3>
    <p>{`If you set the Adobe Experience Platform SDK log level to trace:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-kotlin"
      }}>{`MobileCore.setLogLevel(LoggingMode.VERBOSE)
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`you can then inspect the app logs to confirm that extension shared states are being set with the expected values.`}</p>
    <p>{`Brand Concierge expects the following keys to be present in the Configuration shared state:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li"><inlineCode parentName="strong">{`concierge.server`}</inlineCode></strong>{`: String (server host or base domain used by Brand Concierge requests)`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li"><inlineCode parentName="strong">{`concierge.configId`}</inlineCode></strong>{`: String (datastream ID)`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p>{`The ECID is read from the Edge Identity shared state.`}</p>
    <p>{`Another option for validation is to use Adobe Assurance. Refer to the `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "/aep-mobile-sdkdocs/src/pages/home/getting-started/validate/"
      }}>{`Mobile SDK validation guide`}</a>{` for more information.`}</p>
    <hr></hr>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "integration"
    }}>{`Integration`}</h2>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "managed-integration"
    }}>{`Managed Integration`}</h3>
    <p>{`Use this when you want to provide a drop-in entry point to the chat interface and let the Brand Concierge extension automatically manage it.`}</p>
    <p>{`This mode:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Shows the configured trigger when the Brand Concierge extension is ready (ECID and Concierge configuration available)`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Opens chat as a full-screen dialog when the trigger is invoked`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Handles dismissal (back press, close button, gestures) automatically`}</li>
    </ul>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "jetpack-compose"
    }}>{`Jetpack Compose`}</h4>
    <p>{`The `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`ConciergeChat`}</inlineCode>{` composable can be configured with a UI element (button, floating action button, or any custom element) of your choice to act as a trigger to launch the chat interface. Pass the list of surface URLs via `}<strong parentName="p"><inlineCode parentName="strong">{`surfaces`}</inlineCode></strong>{`.`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-kotlin"
      }}>{`@Composable
fun MyScreen() {
    val viewModel = viewModel<ConciergeChatViewModel>()
    val surfaces = listOf("web://example.com/your-surface.html")
    
    // Your app content
    // ... other views ...
    
    ConciergeChat(
        viewModel = viewModel,
        surfaces = surfaces
    ) { showChat ->
        // Your trigger button/view that launches ConciergeChat
        MyTriggerButton(onClick = { showChat() }) 
    }
}
`}</code></pre>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "xmlviews"
    }}>{`XML/Views`}</h4>
    <p>{`For non-Compose apps, the SDK provides `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`ConciergeChatView`}</inlineCode>{` that wraps the Compose chat UI and can be included in XML layouts.`}</p>
    <blockquote>
      <p parentName="blockquote"><strong parentName="p">{`Note:`}</strong>{` The activity hosting `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`ConciergeChatView`}</inlineCode>{` must have `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`android:windowSoftInputMode="adjustResize"`}</inlineCode>{` added in the app's `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`AndroidManifest.xml`}</inlineCode>{` to ensure the chat input field remains visible when the keyboard is shown:`}</p>
      <pre parentName="blockquote"><code parentName="pre" {...{
          "className": "language-xml"
        }}>{`<activity
    android:name=".YourActivity"
    android:windowSoftInputMode="adjustResize" />
`}</code></pre>
    </blockquote>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Step 1: Add the view to your XML layout`}</strong></p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-xml"
      }}>{`<com.adobe.marketing.mobile.concierge.ui.chat.ConciergeChatView
    android:id="@+id/concierge_chat"
    android:layout_width="match_parent"
    android:layout_height="wrap_content" />
`}</code></pre>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Step 2: Bind with a trigger view in your Activity/Fragment`}</strong></p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-kotlin"
      }}>{`class XmlActivity : AppCompatActivity() {
    override fun onCreate(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
        super.onCreate(savedInstanceState)
        setContentView(R.layout.activity_main)
        
        // Load optional theme
        val theme = ConciergeThemeLoader.load(this, "myTheme.json")
        
        // Create a trigger button of your choice
        val triggerButton = Button(this).apply {
            text = "Start Chat"
            textSize = 18f
            setPadding(32, 16, 32, 16)
        }
        
        // Obtain the ConciergeChatView and bind the triggerButton
        val chatView = findViewById<ConciergeChatView>(R.id.concierge_chat)
        val surfaces = listOf("web://example.com/your-surface.html")
        chatView.bind(
            lifecycleOwner = this,
            viewModelStoreOwner = this,
            surfaces = surfaces,
            theme = theme,  // Optional: apply custom theme
            triggerView = triggerButton
        )
    }
}
`}</code></pre>
    <hr></hr>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "custom-integration"
    }}>{`Custom Integration`}</h3>
    <p>{`Use this when you want to embed the chat interface directly into your app's view hierarchy for more flexibility. This is useful for dedicated chat screens or custom layouts.`}</p>
    <p>{`In this mode, you should:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Ensure that the necessary components (ECID, Configuration) are ready before making this visible. You can check readiness by observing `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`ConciergeStateRepository.instance.state`}</inlineCode>{`.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Manage the logic to control what happens when the chat interface is dismissed when notified via `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`onClose`}</inlineCode>{`.`}</li>
    </ul>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "jetpack-compose-1"
    }}>{`Jetpack Compose`}</h4>
    <p>{`Set surfaces via `}<strong parentName="p"><inlineCode parentName="strong">{`surfaces`}</inlineCode></strong>{` and ensure the extension is ready (configuration, ECID, and surfaces) before showing the chat.`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-kotlin"
      }}>{`@Composable
fun YourChatScreen() {
    val viewModel = viewModel<ConciergeChatViewModel>()
    val conciergeState by ConciergeStateRepository.instance.state.collectAsStateWithLifecycle()
    val surfaces = listOf("web://example.com/your-surface.html")
    // Set surfaces so they are available for the chat session
    ConciergeStateRepository.instance.setSessionSurfaces(surfaces)
    val ready = conciergeState.configurationReady &&
        conciergeState.experienceCloudId != null &&
        conciergeState.surfaces.isNotEmpty()
    
    if (ready) {
        ConciergeChat(
            viewModel = viewModel,
            onClose = {
                // your logic on close or back press
            }
        )
    } else {
        // Show your intermediate loading state or wait for SDK to be ready
    }
}
`}</code></pre>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "xmlviews-1"
    }}>{`XML/Views`}</h4>
    <blockquote>
      <p parentName="blockquote"><strong parentName="p">{`Note:`}</strong>{` The activity hosting `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`ConciergeChatView`}</inlineCode>{` must have `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`android:windowSoftInputMode="adjustResize"`}</inlineCode>{` added in the app's `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`AndroidManifest.xml`}</inlineCode>{` to ensure the chat input field remains visible when the keyboard is shown:`}</p>
      <pre parentName="blockquote"><code parentName="pre" {...{
          "className": "language-xml"
        }}>{`<activity
    android:name=".YourActivity"
    android:windowSoftInputMode="adjustResize" />
`}</code></pre>
    </blockquote>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Step 1: Add the view to your XML layout`}</strong></p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-xml"
      }}>{`<com.adobe.marketing.mobile.concierge.ui.chat.ConciergeChatView
    android:id="@+id/concierge_chat"
    android:layout_width="match_parent"
    android:layout_height="match_parent" />
`}</code></pre>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Step 2: Bind in your Activity/Fragment`}</strong></p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-kotlin"
      }}>{`class XmlActivity : AppCompatActivity() {
    override fun onCreate(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
        super.onCreate(savedInstanceState)
        setContentView(R.layout.activity_main)
        
        // Load optional theme
        val theme = ConciergeThemeLoader.load(this, "myTheme.json")
        
        // Obtain the chat view and bind
        val chatView = findViewById<ConciergeChatView>(R.id.concierge_chat)
        val surfaces = listOf("web://example.com/your-surface.html")
        chatView.bind(
            lifecycleOwner = this,
            viewModelStoreOwner = this,
            surfaces = surfaces,
            theme = theme,  // Optional: apply custom theme
            onClose = { finish() }
        )
    }
}
`}</code></pre>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "theme-customization"
    }}>{`Theme Customization`}</h3>
    <p>{`The Brand Concierge chat interface can be customized by loading the theme file from the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`assets`}</inlineCode>{` directory of your app using `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`ConciergeThemeLoader`}</inlineCode>{`.`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-kotlin"
      }}>{`@Composable
fun MyScreen() {
    val context = LocalContext.current

    val theme = remember {
        ConciergeThemeLoader.load(context, "myTheme.json")
            ?: ConciergeThemeLoader.default()
    }
    
    ConciergeTheme(theme = theme) {
        ConciergeChat(/* ... */)
    }
}
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`More information regarding theme customization can be found in the `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "/aep-mobile-sdkdocs/edge/adobe-brand-concierge/android/style-guide/"
      }}>{`Style guide (Android)`}</a>{`.`}</p>
    <hr></hr>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "deep-links-and-app-links"
    }}>{`Deep Links and App Links`}</h3>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "required-manifest-entries"
    }}>{`Required manifest entries`}</h4>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`1. Register your app as an App Link handler (all API levels)`}</strong></p>
    <p>{`Add an `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`<intent-filter>`}</inlineCode>{` with `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`android:autoVerify="true"`}</inlineCode>{` to the activity in your `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`AndroidManifest.xml`}</inlineCode>{` that should handle your domain's URLs. This triggers Android's domain verification against your domain's `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`assetlinks.json`}</inlineCode>{` file, which is what makes your app the verified handler. Without this, the Concierge extension's App Link check will always fall back to the in-app WebView.`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-xml"
      }}>{`<activity android:name=".YourActivity" ...>
    <intent-filter android:autoVerify="true" android:exported="true">
        <action android:name="android.intent.action.VIEW" />
        <category android:name="android.intent.category.DEFAULT" />
        <category android:name="android.intent.category.BROWSABLE" />
        <data android:scheme="https" android:host="yourdomain.com" />
    </intent-filter>
</activity>
`}</code></pre>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`2. Package visibility (Android 11 and higher)`}</strong></p>
    <p>{`Add the following `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`<queries>`}</inlineCode>{` block to your `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`AndroidManifest.xml`}</inlineCode>{`. Without it, the Concierge extension cannot use `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`PackageManager.resolveActivity()`}</inlineCode>{` to detect the App Link handler on API 30 or higher, and App Links will silently fall back to the in-app WebView.`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-xml"
      }}>{`<!-- Required for PackageManager.resolveActivity() on Android 11+ to detect
     which app handles VIEW intents for http/https URLs. -->
<queries>
    <intent>
        <action android:name="android.intent.action.VIEW" />
        <data android:scheme="https" />
    </intent>
    <intent>
        <action android:name="android.intent.action.VIEW" />
        <data android:scheme="http" />
    </intent>
</queries>
`}</code></pre>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "link-handling"
    }}>{`Link handling`}</h4>
    <p>{`The Concierge extension automatically opens links when your app is the verified handler for the URL's domain. If your app is not the handler, the link opens in an in-app WebView overlay.`}</p>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Default link handling flow:`}</strong>{` `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`handleLink`}</inlineCode>{` callback (if provided) → App Link check → WebView overlay.`}</p>
    <p>{`To customize this behavior, provide a `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`handleLink`}</inlineCode>{` callback. Return `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`true`}</inlineCode>{` if your app handled the link; return `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`false`}</inlineCode>{` to fall back to the default behavior (App Link check, then WebView overlay).`}</p>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Compose (ConciergeChat):`}</strong></p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-kotlin"
      }}>{`val context = LocalContext.current
ConciergeChat(
    viewModel = viewModel,
    onClose = { finish() },
    handleLink = { url ->
        try {
            val intent = Intent(Intent.ACTION_VIEW, Uri.parse(url))
            context.startActivity(intent)
            true  // Handled
        } catch (e: ActivityNotFoundException) {
            false  // Fall back to WebView overlay
        }
    }
)
`}</code></pre>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`XML (ConciergeChatView):`}</strong></p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-kotlin"
      }}>{`chatView.bind(
    lifecycleOwner = this,
    viewModelStoreOwner = this,
    surfaces = surfaces,
    theme = theme,
    handleLink = { url ->
        try {
            val intent = Intent(Intent.ACTION_VIEW, Uri.parse(url))
            startActivity(intent)
            true
        } catch (e: ActivityNotFoundException) {
            false
        }
    },
    onClose = { finish() }
)
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`To close the chat when a deep link is tapped, call `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`viewModel.closeConcierge()`}</inlineCode>{` inside your `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`handleLink`}</inlineCode>{` callback before returning `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`true`}</inlineCode>{`:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-kotlin"
      }}>{`ConciergeChat(
    viewModel = viewModel,
    handleLink = { url ->
        if (url.startsWith("myapp://")) {
            viewModel.closeConcierge()
            // navigate to the deeplink destination
            true
        } else {
            false
        }
    }
) { showChat -> ... }
`}</code></pre>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "in-app-webview-overlay-link-handling"
    }}>{`In-app WebView overlay link handling`}</h4>
    <p>{`Links clicked inside the in-app WebView overlay (for example, links on a product page) follow their own routing rules, independent of the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`handleLink`}</inlineCode>{` callback:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`http/https URLs`}</strong>{`: If your app is the verified App Link handler for the domain, the URL is forwarded to your app. Otherwise it loads in the WebView.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Non-web schemes`}</strong>{` (for example, `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`mailto:`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`tel:`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`sms:`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`myapp://`}</inlineCode>{`): Forwarded to the system via `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`Intent.ACTION_VIEW`}</inlineCode>{`.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Dangerous schemes`}</strong>{` (`}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`javascript:`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`file:`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`content:`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`intent:`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`data:`}</inlineCode>{`): Blocked.`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p>{`No additional configuration is required for this behavior. App Link forwarding within the WebView uses the same domain verification as chat message link handling.`}</p>

      "id": "next-steps"
    }}>{`Next steps`}</h2>

          "href": "/aep-mobile-sdkdocs/edge/adobe-brand-concierge/android/style-guide/"
        }}>{`Style guide (Android)`}</a>{` — Theme JSON reference and implementation status for Android.`}</li>
    </ul>

    </MDXLayout>;
}
;
